login.metaDescription: Sign in to your HR account to manage your HR processes, employees, and payroll.

signUp.metaDescription: Create your HR account and start managing your HR processes in minutes.

systemStatus.metaDescription: Check the real-time status of all HR services.

hero.heading: System Status
hero.subheading: Real-time status for all HR services and infrastructure.

services.title: Service Status
services.description: Current status of all platform services.

search.title: Search Engine
search.description: Full-text search for jobs, candidates, and documents

fileStorage.title: File Storage
fileStorage.description: Document storage, CV uploads, and file management

incidents.title: Active Incidents
incidents.description: Any ongoing incidents will be listed here.

incident1.title: API Latency Spike
incident1.description: Elevated API response times due to database load. Issue identified and resolved.

incident2.title: Email Delivery Delay
incident2.description: Email notifications delayed by up to 30 minutes due to third-party provider issues.

incident3.title: Planned Maintenance
incident3.description: Scheduled database maintenance window. Read-only mode during maintenance.

subscribe.title: Subscribe to Updates
subscribe.description: Get notified about incidents and maintenance windows.

contact.title: Report an Issue
contact.description: Is something wrong? Let us know.

terms.metaDescription: Read the Terms of Service for the HR platform. Governed by Bulgarian law.

hero.heading: Terms of Service
hero.subheading: Please read these terms carefully before using the HR platform.

introduction.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
introduction.content: HR EOOD (UIC 123456789), a company registered under the Bulgarian Commerce Act (Targovki zakon), with registered office at 1 Business Park Sofia, Sofia 1766, Bulgaria ("Company", "we", "us", "our"), operates the HR platform and related services. These Terms of Service ("Terms") constitute a legally binding agreement between you and the Company.
introduction.acceptance: By accessing or using our platform, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms, in compliance with the Bulgarian Electronic Commerce Act (Zakon za elektronnata targoviya). If you do not agree, you must not use our services. These Terms apply to all visitors, users, and paying subscribers.

services.title: 2. Description of Service
services.description: HR provides a cloud-based human resources management platform ("Service") that includes, but is not limited to:

items.item1: Applicant Tracking System (ATS) with AI-powered candidate matching
items.item2: Employee database management and self-service portal
items.item3: Leave tracking with automated approvals and balance calculations
items.item4: Payroll processing compliant with Bulgarian NRA (National Revenue Agency) requirements

services.scope: The Service is designed for Bulgarian businesses and complies with Bulgarian labor law (Kodeks na truda) and applicable EU regulations. Features may vary by subscription plan.

account.title: 3. User Accounts
account.description: Account creation and management are subject to the following terms:

registration.title: Registration
registration.content: You must provide accurate, complete, and current information when creating your account, including your legal company name and UIC (Unified Identification Code) where applicable. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. Each user must have a separate account; shared accounts are not permitted.

termination.title: Account Termination
termination.content: You may close your account at any time through your account settings. Upon termination, we will retain your data for the statutory period required by Bulgarian tax and accounting legislation (currently 5 years for financial records under the Accountancy Act / Zakon za schetovodstvoto) before secure deletion.

responsibilities.title: 4. Acceptable Use
responsibilities.description: As a user of HR, you agree to use the platform responsibly and in accordance with all applicable laws:

accuracy.title: Data Accuracy
accuracy.content: You must ensure that all data entered into the platform, including employee records, job postings, and payroll information, is accurate and up to date. Inaccurate data may lead to non-compliance with Bulgarian labor regulations.

compliance.title: Legal Compliance
compliance.content: You must comply with all applicable laws and regulations, including Bulgarian labor law (Kodeks na truda), the Anti-Discrimination Act (Zakon za zashtita ot diskriminatsiya), and EU employment directives when using the platform for recruitment and HR management.

security.title: Account Security
security.content: You are responsible for keeping your login credentials secure and for all activity under your account. You must immediately notify us at security@hr.com of any unauthorized access or security breach.

prohibited.title: Prohibited Activities
prohibited.description: You may not use the Service to:

items.spam: Send unsolicited communications or spam through the platform
items.malware: Upload malicious software, viruses, or harmful code
items.scraping: Scrape, crawl, or extract data from the platform using automated tools
items.impersonation: Impersonate another person, organization, or entity
items.illegal: Engage in any activity that violates Bulgarian or EU law, including discriminatory hiring practices

intellectual.title: 5. Intellectual Property
intellectual.description: Intellectual property rights are protected under Bulgarian Copyright and Related Rights Act (Zakon za avtorskoto pravo i srodnite mu prava) and applicable international treaties.

ours.title: Our Property
ours.content: The HR platform, including its software, design, logos, documentation, and all related materials, is the exclusive intellectual property of HR EOOD. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Service during your subscription period. You may not copy, modify, reverse-engineer, or distribute any part of the platform.

yours.title: Your Content
yours.content: All data and content you upload to the platform (employee records, documents, job postings) remains your property. By uploading content, you grant us a limited license to process, store, and display it solely for the purpose of providing the Service. This license terminates when you delete the content or close your account.

fees.title: 6. Payment Terms
fees.description: Paid subscriptions are governed by the following terms, in compliance with Bulgarian consumer protection and e-commerce legislation:

subscription.title: Subscription Plans
subscription.content: Paid plans are billed monthly or annually in advance. Prices are displayed on our Pricing page and are quoted in Bulgarian Lev (BGN) or Euro (EUR). All prices are exclusive of 20% Bulgarian VAT (DDS), which will be added where applicable.

refunds.title: Refunds & Cancellation
refunds.content: We offer a 60-day free trial for new accounts. After the trial, payments are non-refundable except where required by the Bulgarian Consumer Protection Act (Zakon za zashtita na potrebitelite). You may cancel your subscription at any time; service will continue until the end of the current billing period.

taxes.title: Taxes & Invoicing
taxes.content: All invoices comply with Bulgarian VAT Act (Zakon za danak varhu dobavenata stoynost) requirements. Electronic invoices are issued automatically upon payment and are available in your account. Bulgarian entities must provide a valid UIC and VAT number for proper invoicing.

privacy.title: 7. Data Processing
privacy.description: We take data protection seriously. As a data processor under GDPR and the Bulgarian Personal Data Protection Act (Zakon za zashtita na lichnite danni), we process personal data only as necessary to provide the Service and in accordance with our Data Processing Agreement.
privacy.policyLink2: and GDPR Compliance page for full details on how we collect, process, and protect your data.

liability.title: 8. Limitation of Liability
liability.description: To the maximum extent permitted by Bulgarian law and the Bulgarian Obligations and Contracts Act (Zakon za zadazheniyata i dogovorite):

items.item1: We are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages, including loss of profit or data
items.item2: Our total aggregate liability shall not exceed the amounts paid by you to us in the 12 months preceding the claim
items.item3: We are not responsible for the actions or omissions of third-party service providers integrated with the platform
items.item4: We are not liable for service interruptions caused by force majeure events as defined under Bulgarian law

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.content: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless HR EOOD, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising from your use of the platform, violation of these Terms, or infringement of any third-party rights.

termination.title: 9. Termination
termination.description: Either party may terminate this agreement under the following conditions:

byUser.title: Termination by You
byUser.content: You may terminate your account at any time through your account settings. Upon termination, you may export your data within 30 days. After this period, data will be securely deleted except where retention is required by law.

byUs.title: Termination by Us
byUs.content: We may suspend or terminate your access immediately if you materially breach these Terms, fail to pay subscription fees after 30 days, use the platform for illegal activities, or pose a security risk to other users. We will provide reasonable notice except in cases of serious breach.

governing.title: 10. Governing Law
governing.content: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Bulgaria, including the Commerce Act (Targovki zakon), the Electronic Commerce Act (Zakon za elektronnata targoviya), and applicable EU regulations including Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).
governing.disputes: Any disputes arising from or in connection with these Terms shall be resolved by the competent courts of the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, unless mandatory consumer protection rules require otherwise. Before initiating legal proceedings, the parties shall attempt to resolve disputes amicably through direct negotiation for a period of at least 30 days.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these Terms from time to time to reflect changes in our services, legal requirements, or business practices. We will notify you at least 30 days before material changes take effect via email and an in-app notification. Your continued use of the Service after the changes take effect constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

contact.title: 11. Contact Information
contact.description: If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us:

summary.title: Summary
summary.content: These Terms govern your use of the HR platform. They are subject to Bulgarian law and designed to protect both parties in a fair and transparent manner.
summary.agreement: If you do not agree with these Terms, please do not use HR.
summary.acceptance: By creating an account, you accept these Terms in full.

privacy.metaDescription: Learn how HR collects, uses, and protects your personal data. Compliant with GDPR and Bulgarian Personal Data Protection Act.

hero.heading: Privacy Policy
hero.subheading: How we collect, use, and protect your personal data in compliance with GDPR and Bulgarian law.

introduction.title: 1. Introduction
introduction.content: HR EOOD ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and share your personal data when you use the HR platform and related services.
introduction.scope: This policy applies to all users of the HR platform, including employers, HR administrators, and employees. It has been drafted in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation / GDPR) and the Bulgarian Personal Data Protection Act (Zakon za zashtita na lichnite danni, ZZLD).

controller.title: 2. Data Controller
controller.description: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:

information.title: 3. Data We Collect
information.description: We collect the following categories of personal data, depending on your role and use of the platform:

personal.title: Personal Identification Data
personal.description: Data you provide directly when creating an account or using the service:

items.name: Full name, job title, and organizational role
items.company: Company name, UIC, and VAT number

usage.title: Usage & Technical Data
usage.description: Data collected automatically when you interact with the platform:

items.device: Device type, operating system, and screen resolution
items.browser: Browser type and version, IP address

employment.title: Employment Data
employment.description: Data processed on behalf of your employer through the HR platform:

items.cv: CVs, cover letters, and application materials
items.leave: Leave balances, requests, and attendance records

usage.title: 4. How We Use Data
usage.description: We process your personal data for the following purposes:

items.service: Providing and maintaining the HR platform and its features
items.support: Responding to your inquiries and providing technical support
items.improvement: Analyzing usage patterns to improve our services and user experience
items.communication: Sending service-related notifications, updates, and (with consent) marketing communications
items.security: Detecting and preventing fraud, abuse, and security threats
items.legal: Complying with legal obligations under Bulgarian and EU law, including tax reporting to the NRA

legalBasis.title: 5. Legal Basis (GDPR Article 6)
legalBasis.description: We process your personal data based on the following legal grounds as defined in Article 6(1) of the GDPR:

consent.title: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
consent.description: For marketing communications, non-essential cookies, and analytics. You can withdraw consent at any time through your account settings or our cookie preferences.

contract.title: Contractual Necessity (Art. 6(1)(b))
contract.description: Processing necessary to perform our contract with you, including account management, service delivery, billing, and customer support.

legal.title: Legal Obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))
legal.description: Processing required to comply with Bulgarian tax law (ZDDFL, ZKPO), the Accountancy Act, labor law (Kodeks na truda), and social security reporting obligations.

interest.title: Legitimate Interest (Art. 6(1)(f))
interest.description: Processing necessary for our legitimate business interests, including platform security, fraud prevention, service improvement, and direct marketing to existing customers. We conduct a balancing test to ensure your rights are not overridden.

sharing.title: 6. Data Sharing
sharing.description: We are committed to keeping your data confidential. We share personal data only in limited circumstances:

never.title: What We Never Do
never.content: We never sell your personal data to third parties. We never share employee data with other employers or advertisers. We never use your data for purposes unrelated to our service.

exceptions.title: When We May Share Data
exceptions.description: We may share your data in the following limited cases:

items.consent: With your explicit consent for specific purposes
items.providers: With trusted service providers (sub-processors) who help operate our platform, under strict data processing agreements
items.legal: When required by Bulgarian or EU law, court order, or regulatory request (e.g., NRA, CPDP)
items.safety: To protect the safety, rights, or property of our users or the public
items.transfer: In the event of a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with prior notice to affected users

international.title: 7. International Transfers
international.description: Your data is primarily stored within the European Union (AWS Frankfurt region). When data transfer outside the EU/EEA is necessary, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place.
international.safeguards: Transfer safeguards include: EU adequacy decisions, Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, and supplementary measures as recommended by the EDPB. We do not transfer data to countries without adequate protection unless such safeguards are in place.

retention.title: 8. Data Retention
retention.description: We retain your personal data only for as long as necessary for the purposes described in this policy:

items.active: Account data: retained for the duration of your subscription plus 30 days for data export
items.legal: Financial records: retained for 5 years as required by the Bulgarian Accountancy Act (Zakon za schetovodstvoto) and 10 years for tax documentation
items.deletion: Upon account deletion: personal data is anonymized or deleted within 30 days, except where legal retention periods apply

rights.title: 9. Your Rights
rights.description: Under GDPR and the Bulgarian Personal Data Protection Act, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

access.title: Right of Access (Art. 15)
access.content: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you, including the purposes of processing and categories of recipients.

correction.title: Right to Rectification (Art. 16)
correction.content: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. You can update most information directly through your account settings.

deletion.title: Right to Erasure (Art. 17)
deletion.content: Request deletion of your personal data when it is no longer necessary for the original purpose, subject to legal retention requirements.

portability.title: Right to Data Portability (Art. 20)
portability.content: Receive your personal data in a structured, machine-readable format (JSON/CSV) and transfer it to another service provider.

restriction.title: Right to Restriction (Art. 18)
restriction.content: Request that we limit processing of your data while a dispute is being resolved or while accuracy is being verified.

objection.title: Right to Object (Art. 21)
objection.content: Object to processing based on legitimate interest or direct marketing at any time. We will stop processing unless we demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds.

rights.exerciseNote: To exercise any of these rights, contact our DPO at dpo@hr.com. We will respond within 30 days (extendable by 60 days for complex requests) as required by GDPR. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Commission for Personal Data Protection (CPDP) at www.cpdp.bg.

children.title: 10. Children's Privacy
children.content: HR is a business-to-business platform and is not intended for use by individuals under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe we have inadvertently collected data from a minor, please contact us immediately at privacy@hr.com and we will delete it promptly.

changes.title: 11. Changes to Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy periodically to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or other factors. We will notify you of material changes at least 30 days in advance via email and/or in-app notification. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this policy regularly.

contact.title: 12. Contact DPO
contact.description: For questions about this Privacy Policy or to exercise your data protection rights, please contact:
contact.supervisoryDetails: Commission for Personal Data Protection (CPDP), Sofia 1592, Blvd. "Prof. Tsvetan Lazarov" No 2, www.cpdp.bg, kzld@cpdp.bg

summary.title: Summary
summary.content: We collect only the data necessary to provide our HR platform services, process it lawfully under GDPR, and protect it with industry-standard security measures. Your rights to access, correct, delete, and port your data are fully supported.
summary.trust: Your trust is the foundation of our business.
summary.commitment: We are committed to transparency and data protection.

cookies.metaDescription: Learn about the cookies and tracking technologies used on the HR platform.

hero.heading: Cookie Policy
hero.subheading: How we use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience.

whatAre.title: 1. What Are Cookies
whatAre.description: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device (computer, tablet, or phone) when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work more efficiently, provide a better user experience, and give website owners useful analytics information.
whatAre.purpose: HR uses cookies and similar technologies (such as local storage and pixel tags) to remember your preferences, keep you logged in, understand how you use our platform, and improve our services. This Cookie Policy should be read together with our Privacy Policy.

types.title: 2. Types of Cookies
types.description: We use four categories of cookies on the HR platform:

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: These cookies are strictly necessary for the platform to function. They enable core features like user authentication, session management, and security. Without these cookies, the platform cannot operate properly.
essential.examples: Cannot be disabled. Required for platform functionality.

performance.title: Analytics / Performance Cookies
performance.description: These cookies help us understand how visitors interact with our platform by collecting anonymous usage data. We use this information to identify popular features, detect issues, and improve the overall experience.
performance.examples: Can be disabled through cookie preferences. Data is anonymized.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.description: These cookies remember your choices and preferences (such as language, theme, and display settings) to provide a more personalized experience. Disabling them may reduce platform functionality.
functional.examples: Can be disabled. Some features may not work optimally.

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
marketing.description: These cookies track your activity across websites to deliver more relevant advertising and measure the effectiveness of our marketing campaigns. They are set by our advertising partners.
marketing.examples: Can be disabled. You may still see ads, but they will be less relevant.

cookieTable.title: 3. Cookies We Use
cookieTable.description: The following table provides details about each cookie used on the HR platform:

security.title: Security & Authentication
security.description: Keeping you safely logged in and protecting against unauthorized access.

performance.title: Performance Monitoring
performance.description: Understanding page load times and identifying performance bottlenecks.

personalization.title: Personalization
personalization.description: Remembering your language, theme, and display preferences.

analytics.title: Analytics & Improvement
analytics.description: Understanding which features are used most to prioritize development.

managing.title: 4. How to Manage Cookies
managing.description: You have full control over cookies on the HR platform. You can manage your preferences in several ways:

browser.title: Browser Settings
browser.description: Most browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings. You can block or delete cookies at any time.

consent.title: Cookie Consent Banner
consent.description: When you first visit HR, a cookie consent banner allows you to accept or reject non-essential cookies. You can change your preferences at any time by clicking the cookie settings link in the footer.

choices.title: Your Choices
choices.description: You can opt out of non-essential cookies at any time.

impact.title: Impact of Disabling Cookies
impact.description: Disabling essential cookies will prevent you from logging in and using the platform. Disabling analytics cookies means we cannot track usage patterns to improve the service. Disabling marketing cookies means ads you see may be less relevant to your business needs.

thirdParty.title: 5. Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.description: Some cookies on our platform are set by third-party services we use. These providers have their own privacy policies governing data collection:

analytics.description: Used to collect anonymous usage statistics. Data is processed in compliance with Google's EU data processing terms.

marketing.description: Used to track ad conversions and build audience segments for remarketing campaigns.

linkedin.description: Used to track conversions from LinkedIn advertising campaigns and provide audience insights.

session.title: Session Cookies
session.description: Deleted automatically when you close your browser. Used for login sessions and CSRF protection.

persistent.title: Persistent Cookies
persistent.description: Remain on your device for a set period (days to years). Used for preferences, analytics, and marketing.

updates.title: 6. Changes to Policy
updates.content: We may update this Cookie Policy when we change the cookies we use or when regulations require it. Material changes will be notified via the cookie consent banner. The last update date is shown at the top of this page.

contact.title: 7. Contact
contact.description: If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact:

gdpr.metaDescription: Learn about HR's commitment to GDPR compliance, data protection measures, and your rights as a data subject.

hero.heading: GDPR Compliance
hero.subheading: Our commitment to protecting your personal data under EU and Bulgarian data protection law.

overview.title: 1. Our Commitment
overview.description: HR EOOD is fully committed to compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation / GDPR) and the Bulgarian Personal Data Protection Act (Zakon za zashtita na lichnite danni, ZZLD). As both a data controller and data processor, we implement comprehensive measures to protect personal data throughout its lifecycle.

keyPrinciples.title: GDPR Core Principles We Follow (Article 5)
keyPrinciples.lawfulness: Lawfulness -- We process data only with a valid legal basis (Article 6)
keyPrinciples.fairness: Fairness -- We process data in ways data subjects would reasonably expect
keyPrinciples.transparency: Transparency -- We clearly communicate what data we collect and why
keyPrinciples.purpose: Purpose Limitation -- We collect data only for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes
keyPrinciples.minimization: Data Minimization -- We collect only the data necessary for each purpose
keyPrinciples.accuracy: Accuracy -- We keep personal data accurate and up to date
keyPrinciples.limitation: Storage Limitation -- We retain data only as long as necessary
keyPrinciples.integrity: Integrity & Confidentiality -- We protect data with appropriate technical and organizational measures

dpa.title: 2. Data Processing Agreement
dpa.description: When HR processes personal data on behalf of our customers (as a data processor under Article 28 GDPR), we enter into a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) that establishes clear obligations and safeguards:

scope.title: Scope of Processing
scope.description: The DPA covers all personal data processed through the HR platform, including employee records, recruitment data, payroll information, and HR documents. Processing is limited to the purposes specified in the service agreement.

duration.title: Duration & Termination
duration.description: The DPA remains in effect for the duration of the service agreement. Upon termination, we will return or securely delete all personal data within 30 days, unless retention is required by Bulgarian law (e.g., Accountancy Act retention periods).

obligations.title: Processor Obligations
obligations.description: We process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, ensure all personnel with access are bound by confidentiality, implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures, assist with data subject requests, and support compliance assessments.

subProcessors.title: 3. Sub-Processors
subProcessors.description: We use the following sub-processors to deliver our services. Each sub-processor is bound by a data processing agreement and has been assessed for GDPR compliance:

security.title: 4. Technical & Organizational Measures
security.description: We implement comprehensive security measures as required by Article 32 of the GDPR to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems:

measures.title: Security Measures (Article 32 GDPR)
measures.encryption: Encryption: TLS 1.3 for data in transit, AES-256 for data at rest in our EU-based infrastructure
measures.access: Access Control: Role-based access control (RBAC), multi-factor authentication (MFA), and principle of least privilege
measures.audits: Auditing: Comprehensive audit logging of all data access and modifications, with tamper-proof log storage
measures.training: Staff Training: Annual GDPR and data protection training for all employees, with specialized training for the development team
measures.backup: Business Continuity: Automated encrypted backups with point-in-time recovery, stored in geographically separate EU data centers
measures.monitoring: Monitoring: 24/7 security monitoring with automated threat detection and incident response procedures

rights.title: 5. Data Subject Rights
rights.description: Under Chapter III of the GDPR, data subjects have the following rights. We facilitate the exercise of all these rights through our platform and DPO:

inform.title: Right to Be Informed (Articles 13-14)
inform.description: You have the right to clear, transparent information about how your personal data is collected and used. This GDPR page and our Privacy Policy fulfill this obligation.

access.title: Right of Access (Article 15)
access.description: You can request a complete copy of all personal data we hold about you, including processing purposes, data categories, recipients, and retention periods.

rectification.title: Right to Rectification (Article 16)
rectification.description: You can request correction of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. Most data can be updated directly through your account settings.

erasure.title: Right to Erasure (Article 17)
erasure.description: You can request deletion of your personal data when processing is no longer necessary, you withdraw consent, or processing is unlawful. Subject to legal retention requirements under Bulgarian law.

restrict.title: Right to Restriction (Article 18)
restrict.description: You can request that processing of your data be restricted while accuracy is verified, processing legality is contested, or we no longer need the data but you require it for legal claims.

portability.title: Right to Data Portability (Article 20)
portability.description: You can receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format (JSON or CSV) and transmit it to another controller.

objection.title: Right to Object (Article 21)
objection.description: You can object to processing based on legitimate interests or for direct marketing purposes at any time. We will cease processing unless we demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds.

automated.title: Automated Decision-Making (Article 22)
automated.description: You have the right not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects. Our AI features (e.g., candidate matching) provide recommendations only -- final decisions are always made by humans.

exercise.title: How to Exercise Your Rights
exercise.description: We have established a streamlined process for handling data subject requests:

process.title: Request Process
process.step1: Send your request to dpo@hr.com with your full name and account email
process.step2: We will verify your identity within 3 business days
process.step3: Your request will be processed within 30 calendar days (Article 12(3) GDPR)
process.step4: You will receive confirmation and any requested data in your chosen format

timeline.title: Response Timeline
timeline.content: We respond within 30 days. For complex or numerous requests, this may be extended by 60 days with prior notice, as permitted by Article 12(3) GDPR.

transfers.title: 6. Cross-Border Transfers
transfers.description: HR stores all primary data within the European Union (AWS Frankfurt, eu-central-1). When data transfer outside the EU/EEA is necessary for specific services, we rely on the following GDPR-approved transfer mechanisms:

safeguards.title: Transfer Safeguards
safeguards.adequacy: EU Adequacy Decisions (Article 45 GDPR) -- for transfers to countries with equivalent protection
safeguards.sccs: Standard Contractual Clauses (Article 46(2)(c) GDPR) -- approved by European Commission Decision 2021/914, with supplementary measures per EDPB Recommendations 01/2020
safeguards.bcrs: Binding Corporate Rules (Article 47 GDPR) -- where applicable for multinational sub-processors
safeguards.derogations: Article 49 Derogations -- only as a last resort, with explicit consent and individual risk assessment

notification.title: Notification Obligations
notification.content: In the event of a personal data breach likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, we will: (1) notify the Commission for Personal Data Protection (CPDP) within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach (Article 33); (2) notify affected data subjects without undue delay if the breach is likely to result in high risk (Article 34); (3) document all breaches in our internal breach register regardless of risk level.

compliance.title: 9. Compliance Documentation
compliance.description: We maintain the following documentation to demonstrate GDPR compliance (Article 5(2) accountability principle):

items.ropa: Records of Processing Activities (ROPA) as required by Article 30
items.dpia: Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA) for high-risk processing operations (Article 35)
items.policies: Internal data protection policies and procedures, reviewed annually
items.training: Staff training records and annual compliance certifications
items.incidents: Data breach register and incident response documentation

cookies.title: Cookies & Tracking
cookies.description: Our use of cookies and similar tracking technologies is governed by our Cookie Policy.

controller.title: Data Controller
controller.description: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:

dataTypes.title: Types of Data We Process
dataTypes.description: We process various categories of personal data depending on your relationship with us:

personal.title: Personal Identification Data
personal.description: Basic personal information provided during registration and use:

sensitive.title: Special Category Data (Article 9)
sensitive.description: Processed only with explicit consent or when necessary for employment law obligations under Bulgarian labor code:

items.health: Health data (e.g., sick leave certificates) -- processed under Art. 9(2)(b) for employment obligations
items.disability: Disability information -- processed for reasonable accommodation requirements under Bulgarian law
items.ethnicity: Ethnic origin -- only if voluntarily provided for diversity monitoring
items.religion: Religious beliefs -- only for holiday preference management if voluntarily provided

technical.title: Technical Data
technical.description: Automatically collected when you use our platform:

items.location: Approximate geographic location derived from IP

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.description: We process personal data based on the legal grounds established in Article 6 of the GDPR:

consent.title: Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
consent.description: For marketing emails, non-essential cookies, and voluntary data collection.

contract.title: Contractual Necessity (Art. 6(1)(b))
contract.description: For service delivery, account management, billing, and customer support.

legal.title: Legal Obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))
legal.description: For tax reporting, labor law compliance, and regulatory requirements.

interest.title: Legitimate Interest (Art. 6(1)(f))
interest.description: For platform security, fraud prevention, and service improvements.

dpo.title: 7. Data Protection Officer
dpo.description: HR has appointed a Data Protection Officer (DPO) in accordance with Article 37 of the GDPR. The DPO oversees our data protection strategy and monitors compliance:
dpo.name: Name

contact.title: 10. Contact & Complaints
contact.description: For any questions about our GDPR compliance or to exercise your data protection rights:

us.title: Contact Us
us.description: Our Data Protection Officer is available to assist you with any data protection inquiries.

supervisory.title: Supervisory Authority
supervisory.description: If you are not satisfied with our response, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Bulgarian supervisory authority:

summary.title: Our GDPR Commitment
summary.content: HR EOOD is committed to full compliance with GDPR and Bulgarian data protection law. We implement technical and organizational measures that exceed minimum requirements, conduct regular compliance audits, and maintain transparent communication with our users about data processing activities.
summary.commitment: Your data protection is our priority.
summary.promise: We continuously review and update our practices to ensure the highest standard of data protection.

salaryCalculator.metaDescription: Calculate net and gross salary in Bulgaria. Includes social security, taxes, and employer costs.
salaryCalculator.heading: Bulgarian Salary Calculator
salaryCalculator.introP1: Calculate net and gross salary with all Bulgarian social security contributions and income tax. Updated for 2026 tax rates.
salaryCalculator.introP2: Perfect for HR professionals, employees, and employers who need accurate salary calculations.
salaryCalculator.insurableNote: Note: Social security calculated on insurable income
salaryCalculator.howItWorksP1: The calculator uses the official Bulgarian tax rates for 2026. Social security contributions are calculated on the insurable income, which is clamped between the minimum and maximum thresholds.
salaryCalculator.howItWorksP2: Income tax is calculated on the gross salary minus employee social security contributions. The universal pension fund applies only to individuals born after 1960.
salaryCalculator.socialSecurityP1: Bulgaria has a mandatory social security system with contributions from both employees and employers. The rates are set annually by the government.
salaryCalculator.socialSecurityP2: The insurable income for 2026 ranges from BGN 710 to BGN 3,750. Contributions above this amount are not calculated.
salaryCalculator.employerCostsP1: When calculating the total cost of employment, employers must consider both the gross salary and their own social security contributions.
salaryCalculator.employerCostsP2: This total cost represents the full financial commitment of hiring an employee in Bulgaria.
salaryCalculator.ctaDesc: Our HR platform handles all Bulgarian payroll calculations automatically, including social security, taxes, and compliance reporting.

leaveCalculator.metaDescription: Calculate leave balances and entitlements according to Bulgarian labor law.
leaveCalculator.heading: Bulgarian Leave Calculator
leaveCalculator.introP1: Calculate annual leave entitlements and balances according to the Bulgarian Labor Code. Updated for 2026 regulations.
leaveCalculator.introP2: Perfect for HR managers and employees who need accurate leave calculations.
leaveCalculator.yearsOfServiceHint: Enter the total years of service with the current employer.

explanation.title: How Leave is Calculated
explanation.standard: Standard annual leave is 20 working days per year.
explanation.additional: Additional days are granted based on years of service and specific working conditions.
explanation.calculation: The calculation follows the Bulgarian Labor Code and collective bargaining agreements.

templates.metaDescription: Free HR templates for Bulgarian businesses - employment contracts, job descriptions, and more.
templates.heading: HR Templates
templates.subheading: Professional, ready-to-use templates for Bulgarian HR teams.

list.name: Employment Contract Template
list.description: A comprehensive employment contract template compliant with the Bulgarian Labour Code, covering all essential clauses from probation periods to termination conditions.
whatsIncluded: Probation period clauses (up to 6 months per Art. 70 Labour Code)
Working hours, overtime, and rest period provisions
howToUse: Download and review the complete template
Fill in employer and employee details in the designated fields
Customize clauses to match your company policies
Review with legal counsel for compliance with current Bulgarian labour legislation
Print two copies — one for employer, one for employee

list.name: Job Description Template
list.description: Professional job description template designed to attract top candidates in the Bulgarian job market.
howToUse: Download and adapt the template for your position

list.name: Leave Policy
list.description: Comprehensive policy for all types of leave according to Bulgarian legislation.
howToUse: Adapt the policy to your company's needs
Ensure compliance with the Labour Code
